feedback.text: Please consider the new Stop Killing Games initiative that is fighting for video games to stay playable offline as opposed to what certain companies are currently doing and able to do - shutting down servers and completely disabling consumers who bought the game from accessing said game by any means. Second issue is also payment processors - Visa and Mastercard, playing a moral agent and disabling payments regarding adult game content, which consist of digital pixels and represent purely fantasy content and art, just like any other media that contains themes that could be consider "problematic". There are games, movies, social media platforms, forums and even books that contain sexual content, violence, taboo topics and of course a lot more. Clearly, these payment processors are not consistent with their action, since they are allowing payments for real adult videos and related content, as well as many other themes that one could find offensive if they would choose to.
